tables.title: ACS Initial Medication Summary
rows: First drug in ALL ACS — give immediately
Preferred P2Y12 inhibitor; avoid with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors
CONTRAINDICATED with prior stroke/TIA, age ≥75, weight <60 kg
Less effective than ticagrelor/prasugrel; use if P2Y12 inhibitor needed and others contraindicated
Titrate to aPTT; use with PCI and fibrinolysis
CONTRAINDICATED: RV infarction, PDE5 inhibitors, SBP <90
CAUTION: delays P2Y12 absorption; reserve for refractory pain after NTG failure
ONLY if SpO₂ <90% or respiratory distress — not routine

tables.title: Nitroglycerin Contraindications in ACS
rows: RV is preload-dependent; NTG reduces preload → catastrophic hypotension
Inferior STEMI with hypotension; check V4R
PDE5 inhibitor use (sildenafil <24 h, tadalafil <48 h)
Further BP reduction risks organ hypoperfusion
Fixed cardiac output; preload reduction causes hypotension

tables.title: STEMI Reperfusion: Primary PCI vs Fibrinolysis
rows: First choice when achievable within 90 min
Onset <12 h AND anticipated PPCI delay >120 min
Active bleeding, stroke <3 months, ICH, INR >1.7, platelets <100K, aortic dissection
Non-PCI center with prolonged transfer time
